name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Northern Arabian Sea — repositioned off eastern Oman coast, ~1,100km from Iran
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
notes: Day 19: CSG-3 remains primary strike platform for Operation Epic Fury. Redirected from Indo-Pacific to 5th Fleet in January 2026. Repositioned to ~1,100km from Iran's coast, expanding stand-off distance after Iranian gunboat harassment. CVW-9 with F-35Cs (VMFA-314) sustaining high-tempo operations. Iran repeatedly claims hits on Lincoln — all denied by Pentagon. Up to 15+ Arleigh Burke destroyers total in the region. P-8 Poseidons from Bahrain augmenting ASW coverage.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Defence Security Asia satellite analysis 3/15, ABC7 3/12, CENTCOM

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Eastern Mediterranean — transiting to Souda Bay, Crete for pierside repairs
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Transiting to Souda Bay, Crete for 1+ week pierside fire damage repairs
notes: Day 19: CRITICAL UPDATE — Ford transiting to Souda Bay, Crete for 1+ week pierside repairs following non-combat fire March 12 in main laundry dryer vent that spread through ventilation ducts. Damage control took 30+ hours. Nearly 200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation; ~600 displaced from berths; one medevac'd. ~1,000 mattresses transferred from USS John F. Kennedy. Greek outlet Kathimerini reports investigators examining whether fire may have been intentionally set — unconfirmed by US Navy. Ford has been deployed ~267 days (since June 2025), approaching post-Vietnam carrier record of 294 days. CVW-8 embarked; flight operations continued uninterrupted during firefighting. Expected to arrive Souda Bay March 18-19. CENTCOM maintained carrier was 'fully operational' throughout.
source: USNI News, Navy Times, Reuters, NYT 3/17, CNN 3/17, Kathimerini, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Atlantic / Eastern Mediterranean — preparing to deploy
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Third carrier deploying — would create unprecedented three-carrier Middle East presence to backfill Ford
notes: Day 19: Bush completed COMPTUEX off Cape Hatteras March 5-6. CVW-7 flew 1,586 sorties during 28-day workup. Multiple sources (Fox News, 19FortyFive) indicate deployment imminent — would backfill Ford during Crete repairs. Commanded by RADM Alexis Walker, CSG-10. CO Capt. Robert Bibeau.
source: USNI News 3/6, Navy Times 3/9, 19FortyFive 3/11, DPI 3/16, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Singapore Strait area — transited March 17, en route to Middle East
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 19: Reuters footage confirmed Tripoli passing through Singapore Strait on March 17. 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) with F-35B Lightning IIs (VMFA-121), MV-22B Ospreys, and attack helicopters. Approximately one week from the operations area. Deployment ordered March 13 by SecDef Hegseth. MEU capable of amphibious assault and maritime platform seizure — relevant to persistent reporting about possible Kharg Island ground operation. Approximately 5,000 total Marines and sailors (ARG + MEU combined).
source: Reuters footage 3/17, USNI News 3/13, Axios 3/13, CNN, Nation Thailand, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: CRITICAL: 2 of 3 ships in Penang, Malaysia (logistics stop) — MCM gap severe
vessels: LCS-16 USS Tulsa (Penang, Malaysia — spotted Mar 15)
LCS-32 USS Santa Barbara (Penang, Malaysia — spotted Mar 15)
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz — SEVERELY DEGRADED
notes: Day 19: CRITICAL MCM GAP. Two of three MCM-configured LCS ships (USS Tulsa/LCS-16 and USS Santa Barbara/LCS-32) were spotted in Penang, Malaysia on March 15 — described as a 'logistics stop' by NAVCENT. USS Canberra (LCS-30) location uncertain. The four Avenger-class minesweepers that previously served in Bahrain were decommissioned and shipped for scrap in January 2026. Washington Institute estimates 16 MCM vessels needed to clear Hormuz; Navy has 7 total. Iran has laid 'a few dozen' mines and retains 80%+ of its small boats and minelayers. Even a ceasefire would not quickly reopen the Strait.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Washington Institute analysis

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 19: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Conducted record 727-day deployment in 2022-2024. Weapons confirmed in use during conflict include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms >7,000 US targets struck, >15,000 combined by Day 14.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 19: Part of expanded ~15+ destroyer presence in region. Operating in Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman supporting Lincoln's expanded destroyer screen. Eastern Med destroyers: USS Roosevelt (DDG-80), USS Bulkeley (DDG-84), USS Oscar Austin (DDG-79 — intercepted Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey using SM-3), USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116).
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea
